answer:
\boxed{A}  

The study found that 22% of patients allergic to a nonubiquitous allergen (epoxy) had total clearance of eczema, compared to only 10% for ubiquitous allergens (rubber chemicals/latex) and 0% for contact urticaria, suggesting prognosis is better when the allergen is less omnipresent. Although the p-value for clearance was not statistically significant, improvement was significantly more frequent in those who changed jobs, which often reduces exposure to ubiquitous allergens. Thus, the prognosis does appear dependent on the omnipresence of the allergen.
